human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for hospital and health-system analytics boundaries, metric-definition rigor, PHI and minimum-necessary controls, payer/provider dependencies, clinical-versus-administrative escalation behavior, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required scenario families for the issue and healthcare-and-care-delivery research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for analytics-reporting-and-disclosure plus healthcare-and-care-delivery are represented and validated.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to hospital metric definition, source hierarchy, denominator and exclusion logic, ADT/encounter/claim reconciliation notes, caveat design, action thresholds, PHI-safe handling, and adjacent-authority escalation rather than generic BI work.

Authority sources cover CMS Hospital IQR/OQR and public reporting, CMS claims and prior authorization workflow, HHS/OCR minimum-necessary controls, ONC and HL7 interoperability data classes, federal eCQI resources, AHRQ Quality Indicators, CDC NHSN, and HCAHPS guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope hospital analysis, ambiguous metric definitions, missing authorization/referral/eligibility/documentation dependencies, ADT-versus-encounter-versus-claim conflict, PHI-sensitive requests, unsupported causal/policy/public claims, clinical and payer boundaries, hospital terminology normalization, tenant adaptation, and formal signoff routing.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; tenant-specific hospital metric definitions, jurisdictional privacy rules, payer contracts, facility reporting posture, and local reporting differences are handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
